shape_decode: cmp,vp,vpc,naive = L,W,W,L -> value_profile AND value_profile_cmplog RESOLVE; cmplog AND naive BLOCK. vp-PRO family: the CMP_MAP distance gradient resolves where I2S substitution + edge-only feedback cannot.
round_note: FINAL (loop-until-dry) re-design. 6 branches CONFIRMED in prior rounds (FROZEN, hypotheses verbatim). 11 came back rule_not_met. This round adds genuinely DIFFERENT measurable angles for the 11, reusing BUILT registry tools the prior rounds had NOT tried on them, and parks the genuinely non-discriminable ones decidable:false (G3).
label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern (vp,vpc resolve / cmp,naive block). Every hypothesis below is the INDEPENDENT verification (G2): it measures what each arm's CORPUS reaches/assembles (operand-distance, parse depth, structural-token content, corpus development/size, fixed-offset operand enrichment), never the resolve label.
round3_postmortem: Round-2 ran value_distance_reached (1 scalar branch confirmed: sqlite3_14437), depth_reach (sqlite3 grammar branches, the 6 confirmed under structural_depth), and a token-assembly test (joint_necessity pair mode, rule tag_lift>=1.0 AND token_density_lift>=1.3). The token-assembly test SCORED but did NOT hold on the 11 here: the DENSITY clause (>=1.3) is the wrong observable -- VP's gradient produces LARGER / more-DEVELOPED winning seeds (a long valid SQL statement, a deep well-formed XML parse), but a long developed seed spreads its tokens over many filler bytes, so per-byte token DENSITY stays ~1 even when absolute structure is far richer. Three NEW angles this round, each a BUILT tool NOT yet tried on these branches: (1) operand_enrichment/byte_freq_ratio for the ONE clean fixed-offset value gate (lcms_2143 'prtr' FOURCC) -- exactly what that tool was built and anchored for, never run on this branch; (2) corpus_size_ratio (joint_necessity's size_lift metric, used WITHOUT the failed density clause) for the sqlite3 grammar/expression branches whose cards explicitly contrast a developed/long winner vs a short malformed-fragment loser -- a DIFFERENT mechanism claim (corpus DEVELOPMENT, not syntax density); (3) depth_reach EXTENDED to libxml2_6436/6687 -- those two are multi-stage parser.c parse-progress gates (well-formed prologue/DOCTYPE reaching a conditional-section/external-subset loop), and depth_reach's amalgamation blind-spot is SPECIFIC to the sqlite3.c single-file build, NOT libxml2's ordinary parser.c, so it is a valid, untried angle there. Two branches are honestly decidable:false (G3) -- libxml2_7143 (negated single-byte NameChar boundary) and libxml2_7317 (default-vs-prefixed namespace structural discriminator) -- because no built corpus-content tool can discriminate their mechanism from luck.

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient lands its corpus closer to a FIXED scalar gate operand than cmplog/naive get -- the genuine value-distance subtype, restricted to the one gate that has an extractable contiguous literal AND on-disk seeds

params.width_caveat: operand width 2 (<<) -> short-operand chance-alignment flag per tool scope_limits; this is the tool's own validated anchor (vpc reaches '<<' dist 0 vs cmp/naive 0.5), so accept the anchor confidence.

hypotheses.prediction: G1: if VP's gradient drives the corpus onto the '<<' shift operator that gates OP_ShiftLeft, the vp/vpc resolving seeds land ON or NEAR '<<' (winner_min ~ 0) while the cmplog/naive blocking seeds stall away from it (distance_gap > 0, winner_closer true). ALTERNATIVES ruled out: (a) plain coverage luck predicts distance_gap ~ 0; (b) an I2S-substitution story predicts cmplog ALSO closes the distance (cmp_min_distance ~ 0) -- cmplog NOT closing discriminates gradient from I2S, consistent with cmplog being a Layer-1 LOSER here.
hypotheses.rule: winner_closer == true AND distance_gap >= 0.25 AND cmp_min_distance >= 0.40

hypotheses.label: value_profile's gradient-built corpus reaches a DEEPER parse/codegen state along the gating call-chain than cmplog/naive -- the structural/derived/grammar subtype where the gate is a parse DEPTH, not a scalar operand

params.rationale: FROZEN verbatim from prior round (confirmed >=1 branch). depth_reach runs each arm's saved seeds through libafl-sqlite3-cov and reads how deep the branch line executes + whether the blocked side is taken, per-seed-normalized. Measures what the corpus REACHED, independent of the resolve label (G2).

hypotheses.prediction: G1: if VP's gradient climbs the parse chain, value_profile's resolving corpus drives the blocker line far deeper / takes the blocked side, while naive's blocking corpus stalls shallow (depth_lift >> 1, winner_deeper true, side_lift > 1). ALTERNATIVE ruled out: 'no real gradient -- VP resolved by a single lucky jump' predicts EQUAL intermediate parse depth (depth_lift ~ 1, winner_deeper false).
hypotheses.rule: winner_deeper == true AND depth_lift >= 1.5 AND side_lift >= 1.5

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient admits seeds that have ASSEMBLED the recognizable SQL/XML structural tokens the gate requires, so its resolving corpus carries more of the gate's structural tokens AND at higher per-byte density than naive's blocking corpus -- the token-assembly subtype

params.rationale: FROZEN verbatim from prior round (confirmed >=1 branch). Counts, per seed, how many supplied structural TOKENS are present (tag count) and token-per-byte DENSITY. Reads corpus token CONTENT (not cov-line depth, not fixed-offset Hamming). On-disk-corpus only -> scored by whichever server OWNS the target.

hypotheses.prediction: G1, discriminating: if VP resolves by ASSEMBLING the required syntax, value_profile's resolving corpus carries markedly more of the gate's tokens than naive's blocking corpus (vp_tags > naive_tags) at higher density. ALTERNATIVE ruled out: 'VP resolved by coverage luck / volume' predicts vp_tags ~ naive_tags and token_density_lift ~ 1. Independent of the resolve label (G2).

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient ENRICHES the resolving corpus, at the fixed gate offset, with bytes equal to (or progressively closer to) the target FOURCC operand, relative to the decoy class the losers carry -- a fixed-offset value-enrichment signal, distinct from the sliding-window min-distance the value_distance test uses

params.rationale: NEW angle, never run on this branch. lcms_2143 is the ONLY clean FIXED-OFFSET value gate in the shape: the 4-byte DeviceClass FOURCC sits at input offsets 0x0c-0x0f; winners spell 'prtr' (70 72 74 72), losers spell the valid-but-different class 'mntr' (6d 6e 74 72). This is EXACTLY operand_enrichment's design point (anchor harfbuzz/5391, fixed-offset i2s/value literal substitution, validated pro median +3.12). data_realities permits byte_freq_ratio here precisely BECAUSE the gate operand is at a fixed offset with same-sized aligned seeds (the realities forbid it ONLY on different-sized structural-assembly gates -- this gate is neither). Owned by sB (lcms on-disk corpus).

hypotheses.prediction: G1, discriminating: if VP's gradient ratchets the DeviceClass dword from 'mntr' toward 'prtr', value_profile's saved corpus is byte-enriched, at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, toward the target FOURCC relative to naive (signed_target_enrich > 0) while NOT enriched toward the decoy (decoy_enrich <= 0). ALTERNATIVES ruled out: (a) coverage luck / no gradient predicts signed_target_enrich ~ 0 (VP's corpus is no more 'prtr'-leaning than naive's, it just happened to flip the gate once); (b) an I2S-substitution story would credit cmplog -- but cmplog is a Layer-1 LOSER here, so VP-only enrichment discriminates the CMP_MAP gradient from I2S. Independent of the resolve label (G2): the tool counts byte frequencies in the saved corpus, never who resolved the blocker.

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient builds a more-DEVELOPED (systematically LARGER) resolving corpus -- the winning seeds are long valid statements that the gradient grew structure into, while the blocking corpus is short malformed fragments -- a corpus-development/size signal, distinct from per-byte token density

params.rationale: NEW mechanism, never the deciding test on these branches. joint_necessity already emits size_lift (median seed size winner/loser); this hypothesis keys the rule on size_lift (corpus DEVELOPMENT), NOT on the token_density_lift clause that FAILED in round 2. The cards make the size contrast explicit and concrete: sqlite3_14589 winner is a 51-byte operator-laden expression vs loser bare 'SELECT 1;'; sqlite3_14675 winners are 139-byte / 115-byte VACUUM statements vs loser a bare 6-byte 'vacuum'; sqlite3_14479/14475 winners are dotted 'M.L.' qualified names vs loser short 'SELECT CC...' fragments. VP's gradient PRESERVES partial-progress seeds, so its corpus accretes development; naive's blocking corpus stays at short malformed fragments. This is corpus DEVELOPMENT, distinct from per-byte syntax density (a 139-byte VACUUM statement has low token density yet is far more developed than a 6-byte fragment). Measures what the corpus is, never who resolved (G2). On-disk corpus -> scored on s4 (sqlite3).

hypotheses.prediction: G1, discriminating: if VP's gradient accretes development, value_profile's resolving seeds are systematically LARGER than naive's blocking seeds (size_lift >= 1.5) AND carry at least as many structural tokens (tag_lift >= 0.5, a weak token co-requirement so a corpus that is merely larger-but-token-EMPTY -- e.g. inflated by garbage padding -- does NOT pass). ALTERNATIVE ruled out: 'VP resolved by luck, not by growing structure' predicts size_lift ~ 1 (winner corpus no more developed than blocker). A DIFFERENT alternative -- 'VP just inflates seed length with random bytes' -- is ruled out by the tag_lift co-requirement: pure inflation would carry no extra tokens. This is NOT a relaxed re-test of round 2's token_density rule: it tests a different metric (size_lift) under a different mechanism claim (development, not density).

hypotheses.label: value_profile's gradient-built corpus reaches a DEEPER, more-complete multi-stage XML parse than naive's -- the winner stays well-formed long enough to enter the conditional-section / external-subset loop the gate sits in, while the loser aborts on an earlier error -- parse-progress DEPTH, applied to the two libxml2 multi-stage parser gates

params.rationale: NEW members for the depth_reach tool, never run on these two. depth_reach was kept to the sqlite3 set in prior rounds because its blind-spot (per-seed line-count cannot cleanly resolve hot/deep lines) is SPECIFIC to the sqlite3.c single-file amalgamation; libxml2 is built from ordinary multi-file sources (parser.c), where the cov image resolves branch-line execution cleanly. libxml2_6436 (xmlParseConditionalSections else-arm, XML_ERR_CONDSEC_INVALID_KEYWORD) and libxml2_6687 (xmlParseExternalSubset loop completion) are textbook parse-DEPTH gates: the winner carries an intact prologue+DOCTYPE that drives the parser INTO the conditional-section/external-subset loop, the loser aborts on an earlier XML error and never reaches the gate's stage. Runs through libafl-libxml2-cov, reads how deep the branch line executes per-seed-normalized. Independent of the resolve label (G2). Owned by sB (libxml2 on-disk corpus).

hypotheses.prediction: G1, discriminating: if VP's gradient drives the corpus to a more-complete parse, value_profile's resolving seeds execute the blocker line / the deep parse stage far more (depth_lift >= 1.5, winner_deeper true, side_lift >= 1.5) while naive's blocking seeds abort shallow and never reach the stage (loser_depth ~ 0). ALTERNATIVE ruled out: 'both corpora reach the same parse stage and only the final keyword/keyword-class differs' predicts depth_lift ~ 1 (equal stage depth). A depth gap that survives is the parse-progress signature; if depth_lift ~ 1 the parse-progress story is refuted for that branch and it falls to fallback.
hypotheses.rule: winner_deeper == true AND depth_lift >= 1.5 AND side_lift >= 1.5

undecidable.reason: libxml2_7143 gates on xmlValidateNmtokensValueInternal taking the !xmlIsDocNameChar TRUE side -- the winning input's leading attribute byte must fall OUTSIDE the accepted NameChar character class. The 'target' is the COMPLEMENT of an accepted set: a single-byte negated character-class boundary. There is no fixed scalar literal to land on (value_distance_reached / operand_enrichment have no target -- 'any byte not in NameChar' is not a value); no multibyte structural token to count or to grow into (joint_necessity token-assembly + corpus_size both have nothing to count -- a one-byte rejected class is neither a token nor a developed structure); and depth_reach cannot discriminate either, since the winner and loser both reach the same validation function -- they differ only by which SIDE of a single-byte class test the leading byte falls on, not by parse depth. No built (and no cleanly proposable) descriptor can discriminate VP's gradient-toward-a-rejected-byte from luck. Honest decidable:false per G3.

libxml2_7317 (xmlNsDumpOutput, cur->prefix!=NULL FALSE side) gates on whether the serialized namespace node is a DEFAULT namespace (xmlns=, prefix==NULL) rather than a PREFIXED one (xmlns:foo=, prefix!=NULL). The discriminator is a STRUCTURAL SHAPE of the parsed tree (a default-vs-prefixed namespace node), not a measurable corpus property: (a) no fixed scalar operand -> value_distance/operand_enrichment blind; (b) the default-namespace token 'xmlns=' vs prefixed 'xmlns:' was already in the token-assembly token list and the test did NOT hold (a corpus that is token-richer in xmlns does not imply it built a PREFIX==NULL node specifically -- both default and prefixed forms contain 'xmlns'); (c) corpus_size does not predict default-vs-prefixed (both can be equally long); (d) depth_reach cannot distinguish either, since loser seeds DO reach xmlNsDumpOutput -- they just arrive carrying a prefixed namespace, so both arms reach the same serializer depth and differ only by the NULL-ness of a single tree field. No built corpus-content tool can discriminate 'constructed a default namespace' from 'constructed a prefixed namespace' from luck. Honest decidable:false per G3 -- this is the structural-presence variant the corpus-content tools are legitimately blind to. NOTE: 7317 remains a member of vp_gradient_structural_token_assembly so the arbiter still TRIES the token test on it first (decision_order); only if that rule_not_met does it fall to this honest decidable:false.

priors_kept: vp_gradient_scalar_operand_distance (KEEP, confirmed >=1 branch) -- carried forward UNCHANGED (member sqlite3_14437, value_distance_reached, same rule).
vp_gradient_structural_depth (KEEP, confirmed >=1 branch) -- carried forward UNCHANGED (6 sqlite3 members, depth_reach, same rule).

vp_gradient_structural_token_assembly (KEEP, confirmed >=1 branch) -- carried forward UNCHANGED (same 14 members, joint_necessity pair-mode, same rule tag_lift>=1.0 AND token_density_lift>=1.3). Re-validates its confirmed branch(es).
new_mechanisms: vp_gradient_fixed_offset_operand_enrichment -- reuses the BUILT operand_enrichment tool (byte_freq_ratio at the fixed gate offset, target 'prtr' vs decoy 'mntr'). Never run on lcms_2143 in prior rounds; it is the one clean fixed-offset value gate in the shape and the exact design point of that tool. Discriminates VP's byte-level enrichment toward the target FOURCC from luck (signed_target_enrich~0) and from I2S (cmplog is a Layer-1 loser).

vp_gradient_corpus_development_size -- reuses BUILT joint_necessity's size_lift metric under a DIFFERENT mechanism claim (corpus DEVELOPMENT / seed size), keyed by size_lift with a tag_lift co-requirement, NOT by the token_density_lift clause that failed in round 2. Targets the sqlite3 grammar/expression branches whose cards explicitly contrast a long developed winner (51/139/115-byte statements) vs a short malformed-fragment loser. The tag_lift co-requirement rules out the 'pure random-byte inflation' alternative; size_lift>=1.5 rules out the 'no gradient, equal development' alternative.

vp_gradient_parse_progress_depth_libxml2 -- reuses BUILT depth_reach with NEW members (libxml2_6436, libxml2_6687). depth_reach was held to sqlite3 before because its line-resolution blind-spot is specific to the sqlite3.c amalgamation; libxml2's ordinary parser.c resolves cleanly, so it is a valid untried angle for the two libxml2 multi-stage parse-progress gates (conditional-section keyword / external-subset loop). Discriminates 'VP reached a deeper parse stage' from 'both arms reach the same stage, only the final token differs' (depth_lift~1).
decidable_false: libxml2_7143_h0 -- single-byte NEGATED NameChar character-class boundary: no scalar literal (target is a set complement), no countable/growable token, and depth_reach cannot distinguish (both arms reach the same validation function, differing only by which side of a one-byte class test). Genuinely non-discriminable for all built tools (G3).

libxml2_7317_h0 -- default-vs-prefixed namespace STRUCTURAL discriminator (prefix==NULL): no scalar operand; token-richness does not imply a prefix-NULL node (both forms contain 'xmlns'); size does not predict default-vs-prefixed; depth_reach cannot distinguish (loser seeds also reach the serializer, carrying a prefixed namespace). Genuinely non-discriminable for all built corpus-content tools (G3). Stays a token-assembly member so the arbiter still TRIES that test first.
data_realities_respected: on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 (s4) and lcms/libxml2/libpng/bloaty (sB). Cross-server invariant honored: NO branch is marked decidable:false for corpus-absence-on-the-running-server reasons -- the lcms operand_enrichment test (sB), the sqlite3 size/depth tests (s4), and the libxml2 depth tests (sB) are each scored by the OWNING server. The two decidable:false branches are parked for MECHANISTIC reasons (no scalar + no token + depth-indistinguishable), not corpus-absence.

I2S is NOT tagged in mutation_op / value_profile is a FEEDBACK not a mutator -> no hypothesis reads a 'gradient event' or 'I2S event'. operand_enrichment counts saved-corpus byte frequencies at a fixed offset; value_distance_reached reads saved corpus operand bytes; depth_reach reads what the corpus's coverage reaches; joint_necessity (token + size modes) counts tokens / measures seed size in the saved corpus. VP's effect is inferred from what the corpus REACHED/ASSEMBLED/GREW, never from a lineage/mutation event.

byte_freq_ratio (operand_enrichment) is applied ONLY to lcms_2143, a FIXED-OFFSET value gate with same-sized aligned seeds (DeviceClass FOURCC at 0x0c-0x0f) -- this is the case the realities PERMIT. It is NOT applied to any structural-assembly gate (different-sized seeds), where the realities forbid fixed-offset alignment; those use size_lift / token_count / value_distance_reached / depth_reach instead.

all hypotheses are INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern (G2): each measures a corpus property (operand proximity, fixed-offset operand enrichment, parse-line depth, structural-token content, seed development/size) -- none reads whether vp/vpc resolved the blocker.
notes: ROUND-3 FINAL. KEPT all THREE prior KEEP hypotheses VERBATIM (vp_gradient_scalar_operand_distance, vp_gradient_structural_depth, vp_gradient_structural_token_assembly) so the 6 confirmed branches re-validate (superset/monotonic). ADDED three NEW discriminating mechanisms, each reusing a BUILT registry tool the prior rounds had NOT applied to these branches: (1) vp_gradient_fixed_offset_operand_enrichment (operand_enrichment/byte_freq_ratio) for the one clean fixed-offset gate lcms_2143; (2) vp_gradient_corpus_development_size (joint_necessity's size_lift, NOT the failed density clause) for the 6 sqlite3 grammar/expression branches; (3) vp_gradient_parse_progress_depth_libxml2 (depth_reach with new members) for libxml2_6436/6687. NO NEW DESCRIPTOR built -- all reuse registry tools. decision_order tries the sharpest available test per branch first and only falls through on rule_not_met / unmeasurable, so a confirmed branch keeps its cleaner prior label. The 11 round-3 targets map as: lcms_2143 -> operand_enrichment; sqlite3_14475/14479/14589/14623/14675/14765 -> structural_depth (prior) then corpus_development_size then token_assembly; libxml2_6436/6687 -> parse_progress_depth then token_assembly; libxml2_6466/6924/6925/6926/6948 stay on token_assembly (charref / xmlns cascade -- token-bearing); HONEST decidable:false for libxml2_7143 (negated NameChar byte boundary) and libxml2_7317 (default-vs-prefixed namespace structural shape). I did NOT invent a mechanism to force a label on the two decidable:false branches -- they are legitimately non-discriminable for every built corpus-content tool, which is the correct G3 outcome for this FINAL round. If the loop is still not dry after this, the remaining rule_not_met branches should be accepted as inconclusive: the VP-gradient mechanism is real but its corpus signature on those specific gates is below what any built tool can resolve.
lcms_scalar_note: lcms_2143 now has its OWN measurable hypothesis (vp_gradient_fixed_offset_operand_enrichment, operand_enrichment, target 'prtr' / decoy 'mntr', offsets 0x0c-0x0f), scored by the owning server sB. This supersedes round-2's note that deferred it under value_distance_reached: operand_enrichment is the sharper fixed-offset test (it was built and anchor-validated for exactly this fixed-offset class-substitution shape), and keeping it as a SEPARATE new hypothesis preserves the byte-identical KEEP of vp_gradient_scalar_operand_distance (whose single member stays sqlite3_14437). If the sB arbiter prefers, lcms_2143 may ALSO be cross-checked under value_distance_reached (operand 'prtr', mode string_hamming, width 4) -- both tests predict the same direction; operand_enrichment is the primary.
